title: pyrus amygdaliformis var. sinaica
intro: Pyrus amygdaliformis var. sinaica, also known as the Bitter Apricot or Chinese Apricot, is a plant species belonging to the Rosaceae family, closely related to apricot trees. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its seeds which contain amygdalin, a compound that breaks down into hydrogen cyanide when ingested. This plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. Herbal preparations of Pyrus amygdaliformis var. sinaica typically involve drying the seeds and making them into tea or capsules, although its use as a medicinal herb requires caution and proper dosage due to the potential toxicity of its compounds.
